User.title: User
User.description: Represents an individual participant or administrator registered in the AssembleiaOnline platform. This entity stores core profile information and a reference to the external authentication system's user ID.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and as a primary identifier.

name.description: The user's display name or full name.

role.description: The role of the user within the application, e.g., 'participant' or 'admin'. Administrators have additional privileges.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user's profile information was updated.

Assembly.title: Assembly
Assembly.description: Represents an online assembly session, managed by an administrator. It includes details about the event and its associated live stream.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Assembly entity.

title.description: The official title or name of the assembly session.

description.description: A detailed description or agenda for the assembly session.

youtubeLiveStreamUrl.description: The URL of the YouTube live stream associated with this assembly.

scheduledStartTime.description: The planned start date and time of the assembly session.

scheduledEndTime.description: The planned end date and time of the assembly session (optional).

status.description: The current status of the assembly (e.g., 'scheduled', 'live', 'completed', 'cancelled').

adminId.description: Reference to the User (admin) who created and manages this assembly. (Relationship: User 1:N Assembly)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the assembly record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the assembly record was updated.

Motion.title: Motion
Motion.description: Represents a specific topic or proposal presented for discussion and voting within an assembly. It defines the details and state of the voting process for this topic.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Motion entity.

assemblyId.description: Reference to the Assembly this motion belongs to. (Relationship: Assembly 1:N Motion)

title.description: The title or subject of the motion.

description.description: A detailed description of the motion, including its purpose or proposed action.

status.description: The current voting status of the motion (e.g., 'draft', 'pending_vote', 'open_for_vote', 'vote_closed', 'approved', 'rejected').

votingStartTime.description: The date and time when voting for this motion officially opens (optional).

votingEndTime.description: The date and time when voting for this motion officially closes (optional).

suggestedAlternativeWording.description: Optional alternative wording or impact suggestions generated by the AI tool to improve clarity or completeness of the motion.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the motion record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the motion record was updated.

Vote.title: Vote
Vote.description: Records an individual participant's choice for a specific motion during an assembly. Ensures one vote per user per motion.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Vote entity.

motionId.description: Reference to the Motion that was voted on. (Relationship: Motion 1:N Vote)

userId.description: Reference to the User who cast this vote. (Relationship: User 1:N Vote)

choice.description: The choice made by the user for the motion (e.g., 'for', 'against', 'abstain').

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the vote was cast.

definition.description: Stores user profile information, accessible by the user themselves or by administrators. The document ID (`userId`) must match `request.auth.uid`.

Marker collection for administrators. Existence of a document at `/roles_admin/{userId}` signifies that `userId` is an admin. Used for Database Access Control (DBAC). Only administrators can write to this collection, and only for other admins.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who is an administrator. This ID corresponds to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores details about an online assembly session. Includes `adminId` for explicit management ownership. Participants can read, only the `adminId` or general admins can manage this document.

params.name: assemblyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the assembly session.

definition.description: Stores motions proposed within a specific assembly. Includes denormalized `adminId` from the parent assembly for authorization independence. Participants can read motions, only the `adminId` (of the assembly) can manage motions.

params.name: assemblyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent assembly.

params.name: motionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the motion within the assembly.

definition.description: Records individual user votes for a motion. The document ID (`userId`) must match `request.auth.uid` to enforce one vote per user per motion. Includes denormalized `assemblyId`, `adminId`, and `motionStatus` for authorization independence and contextual validation (e.g., voting period).

params.name: assemblyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent assembly.

params.name: motionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent motion.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who cast the vote. This ID corresponds to `request.auth.uid`.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence, ensuring security rules are simple, robust, and easily debuggable by minimizing the use of `get()` operations. This is achieved primarily through strategic denormalization and structural segregation.

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization Strategy):**
1.  **Motions:** To allow independent authorization for motions without querying their parent assembly, each `Motion` document at `/assemblies/{assemblyId}/motions/{motionId}` will denormalize the `adminId` from its parent `Assembly`. This means that security rules for motions can directly check `resource.data.adminId == request.auth.uid` for admin-level access, eliminating the need for `get(/assemblies/$(assemblyId))`.
2.  **Votes:** Similarly, `Vote` documents at `/assemblies/{assemblyId}/motions/{motionId}/votes/{userId}` will denormalize `assemblyId`, `adminId` (from the parent `Assembly` via `Motion`), and `motionStatus` (from the parent `Motion`). This allows rules for votes to verify the context (e.g., if the user is voting in the correct assembly, if the motion is 'open_for_vote', or if an admin is accessing the vote count) without any `get()` calls on parent `Assembly` or `Motion` documents.

**QAPs (Query Anything Principle) Support:**
1.  **Structural Segregation:** Separate collections like `/users` for user profiles, `/roles_admin` for administrative role checks, and `/assemblies` for top-level assembly data ensure homogeneous security postures. Publicly readable data (e.g., assembly details, published motions) is clearly separated from private data (e.g., user profiles, individual votes).
2.  **Explicit Status Fields:** The `status` field in `Assembly` and `Motion` entities (e.g., `scheduled`, `live`, `open_for_vote`) allows for efficient and secure list queries. For instance, participants can easily query `db.collection('assemblies').where('status', 'in', ['scheduled', 'live'])` and security rules can validate these queries without needing to filter results. Similarly, participants can list motions for an assembly using `db.collection('/assemblies/{assemblyId}/motions').where('status', 'in', ['pending_vote', 'open_for_vote', 'vote_closed'])`.
3.  **Path-Based Ownership and DBAC:**
    *   `/users/{userId}` ensures that only the `userId` (`request.auth.uid`) or an authorized administrator can access their profile, supporting granular access control for personal data.
    *   `/roles_admin/{userId}` provides a clear and efficient mechanism for Database Access Control (DBAC). Rules can check `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` to determine if a user has administrative privileges, enabling QAPs for admins across collections.
    *   The `adminId` in `Assembly` and denormalized in `Motion` and `Vote` documents, combined with the `/roles_admin` collection, facilitates simple rule logic for allowing admins to perform `list` operations across their managed assemblies and motions, and view vote tallies.

This design ensures that authorization checks are performed directly on the document being accessed, or through a simple existence check for global roles, making the system highly performant, secure, and easy to maintain.
